code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: ONLY a single number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
    - Do NOT output any explanation, text, symbols, or punctuation
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
    - Explicit repurchasing/gifting behavior mentioned despite minor issues →5
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Multiple product failures within short timeframe (≤3 months)
    - Replacement units showing identical defects →1
    - Explicit warnings against purchase + anger/returns
    - Safety hazards requiring immediate discontinuation →1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Creates new problems during operation (odor/health concerns)
    - Underperforms direct competitors despite proper use →2
    - "Works but requires unreasonable workarounds" scenarios →2

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Conflicted comparisons without clear preference →3
    - Temporary solutions needing imminent replacement →3
    - "Adequate but..." statements without enthusiasm →3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Regular maintenance impacts convenience →4
    - Style/design praise with functional compromises →4
    - Explicit star deductions for specific factors →4
    - Long-term praise with explicit limitations →4

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Sustained satisfaction through multiple use cycles →5
    - Maintenance presented as natural/normal product requirement →5
    - Solves persistent problems others couldn't →5
    - Flaws acknowledged but irrelevant to core value proposition →5

    BOUNDARY ENHANCEMENTS:
    - "Still works after X months" + satisfaction →5 vs "Works but degraded" →4
    - Comparative phrasing: "Surpassed cheaper brands" →5 vs "Better than nothing" →4
    - Maintenance context: "Easy to clean" →5 vs "Requires scrubbing" →4
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Purchased another in 2016 - failed in 2 months. New models are junk!"
<Reasoning>Multiple rapid failures with explicit quality condemnation</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Leaves chemical odor while cleaning - creates breathing issues"
<Reasoning>Introduces health-related operational hazards</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Works okay for now but planning to upgrade soon"
<Reasoning>Temporary solution with replacement intent</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "This throw is warm and stylish but nothing extraordinary"
<Reasoning>Positive attributes without transformative impact</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Between pets and kids, this cleaner keeps surprising me years later!"
<Reasoning>Long-term satisfaction through multiple use cycles</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Blender needs weekly deep cleaning but makes perfect smoothies"
<Reasoning>Maintenance accepted as normal for core excellence</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Effective on surfaces but can't handle deep carpet stains"
<Reasoning>Explicit functional limitation despite effectiveness</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Solved my decade-old stain problem others couldn't touch"
<Reasoning>Unique problem-solving despite any requirements</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Machine only freshens carpets - Bissel/Rug Doctor work better"
<Reasoning>Negative comparison with competitors</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Good initial results but unsure about longevity"
<Reasoning>Unproven durability despite positive start</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (39 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 16
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 2 -> 1: 2
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [1, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.50
  2. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "this everyday throw is soft and cuddly and will get lots of use from your family. They will be covering up in this throw because it is warm. It is also nice looking. The chevron pattern is up to date and the colors go with many decors. This throw is ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "These are great as decorative throws. They are much too rough to be used for anything else. I personally like to curl up on the sofa with a "super soft" throw when reading but there is no way this throw is comfortable enough for that. My friend came ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Returned. Only because I decided to go with tiles and not a rug"
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I've never tried an air purifier before, though I know people who have/had them. This one was not impressive. First, it has an odor. I don't know if it's because of impurities that emit a smell once trapped in the gadget and exposed to heat from the ..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 39 errors, 15 borderline out of 80 examples."}
